channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch Zimbra XSS flaw exploited in attacks
item.description: CISA has ordered U.S. government agencies to secure their servers against an actively exploited vulnerability in the Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS). [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ConnectWise patches new flaw allowing ScreenConnect hijacking
item.description: ConnectWise is warning ScreenConnect customers of a cryptographic signature verification vulnerability that could lead to unauthorized access and privilege escalation. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OFAC Sanctions DPRK IT Worker Network Funding WMD Programs Through Fake Remote Jobs
item.description: The U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has sanctioned six individuals and two entities for their involvement in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ransomware gang exploits Cisco flaw in zero-day attacks since January
item.description: The Interlock ransomware gang has been exploiting a maximum severity remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Cisco's Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) software in zero-day attacks since lat...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco FMC Zero-Day CVE-2026-20131 for Root Access
item.description: Amazon Threat Intelligence is warning of an active Interlock ransomware campaign that's exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Marquis: Ransomware gang stole data of 672K people in cyberattack
item.description: Marquis, a Texas-based financial services provider, revealed this week that a ransomware gang stole the data of over 670,000 individuals in an August 2025 cyberattack that also disrupted operations at...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'Claudy Day’ Trio of Flaws Exposes Claude Users to Data Theft
item.description: A prompt injection vulnerability paired with other flaws can turn a Google search into a full attack chain that could threaten enterprise networks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The Refund Fraud Economy: Exploiting Major Retailers and Payment Platforms
item.description: Refund fraud is now a business, with methods and tutorials sold to exploit return policies for profit. Flare shows how fraudsters turn refunds and chargebacks into a repeatable profit model. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New “Darksword” iOS exploit used in infostealer attack on iPhones
item.description: A new exploit kit for iOS devices and delivery framework dubbed "Darksword" has been used to steal a wide range of personal information, including data from cryptocurrency wallet app. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Nordstrom's email system abused to send crypto scams to customers
item.description: Customers of upscale department store chain Nordstrom received fraudulent messages from a legitimate company email address that promoted cryptocurrency scams disguised as a St. Patrick's Day promotion...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SideWinder Espionage Campaign Expands Across Southeast Asia
item.description: The suspected India-linked threat group targets governments, telecom, and critical infrastructure using spear-phishing, old vulnerabilities, and rapidly rotating infrastructure to maintain persistent ...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Researchers: Meta, TikTok Steal Personal & Financial Info When Users Click Ads
item.description: Tracking pixels let social media companies spy on their users even after they click over to advertiser sites, gleaning credit card info, geolocations, and more, according to an analysis....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw (CVE-2026-32746) Enables Unauthenticated Root RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security flaw impacting the GNU InetUtils telnet daemon (telnetd) that could be exploited by an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Claude Code Security and Magecart: Getting the Threat Model Right
item.description: When a Magecart payload hides inside the EXIF data of a dynamically loaded third-party favicon, no repository scanner will catch it – because the malicious code never actually touches your repo. As te...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 9 Critical IP KVM Flaws Enable Unauthenticated Root Access Across Four Vendors
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned about the risks posed by low-cost IP KVM (Keyboard, Video, Mouse over Internet Protocol) devices, which can grant attackers extensive control over compromised hos...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Product Walkthrough: How Mesh CSMA Reveals and Breaks Attack Paths to Crown Jewels
item.description: Security teams today are not short on tools or data. They are overwhelmed by both. Yet within the terabytes of alerts, exposures, and misconfigurations – security teams still struggle to understand c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ubuntu CVE-2026-3888 Bug Lets Attackers Gain Root via systemd Cleanup Timing Exploit
item.description: A high-severity security flaw affecting default installations of Ubuntu Desktop versions 24.04 and later could be exploited to escalate privileges to the root level. Tracked as CVE-2026-3888 (CVSS sco...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apple Fixes WebKit Vulnerability Enabling Same-Origin Policy Bypass on iOS and macOS
item.description: Apple on Tuesday released its first round of Background Security Improvements to address a security flaw in WebKit that affects iOS, iPadOS, and macOS. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20643 (CV...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apple pushes first Background Security Improvements update to fix WebKit flaw
item.description: Apple has released its first Background Security Improvements update to fix a WebKit flaw tracked as CVE-2026-20643 on iPhones, iPads, and Macs without requiring a full operating system upgrade. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: More Attackers Are Logging In, Not Breaking In
item.description: Credential theft soared in the second half of 2025, thanks in part to the industrialization of infostealer malware and AI-enabled social engineering....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GlassWorm malware hits 400+ code repos on GitHub, npm, VSCode, OpenVSX
item.description: The GlassWorm supply-chain campaign has returned with a new, coordinated attack that targeted hundreds of packages, repositories, and extensions on GitHub, npm, and VSCode/OpenVSX extensions. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Clear Communication: The Missing Link in Cybersecurity Success
item.description: When technical expertise meets clear communication, cybersecurity teams thrive. Learn how to foster trust and collaboration across diverse working groups....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Less Lucrative Ransomware Market Makes Attackers Alter Methods
item.description: Ransomware actors are ditching Cobalt Strike in favor of native Windows tools, as payment rates hit record lows and data theft surges....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
item.description: In an unsuccessful phishing attack, threat actors leveraged trusted brands and domains to try to redirect a C-suite executive at Outpost24 to give up his credentials....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Europe sanctions Chinese and Iranian firms for cyberattacks
item.description: The European Union Council has announced sanctions against three entities and two individuals for their involvement in cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure in the region. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Flaws in Amazon Bedrock, LangSmith, and SGLang Enable Data Exfiltration and RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new method for exfiltrating sensitive data from artificial intelligence (AI) code execution environments using domain name system (DNS) queries. I...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Warlock Ransomware Group Augments Post-Exploitation Activities
item.description: In a recent attack, the group showcased stealthier cross-network activity, thanks to its use of a new BYOVD technique and other tools....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: LeakNet Ransomware Uses ClickFix via Hacked Sites, Deploys Deno In-Memory Loader
item.description: The ransomware operation known as LeakNet has adopted the ClickFix social engineering tactic delivered through compromised websites as an initial access method. The use of ClickFix, where users are tr...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Top 5 Things CISOs Need to Do Today to Secure AI Agents
item.description: AI agents are autonomous actors with real access to data and systems, not just copilots. Token Security explains why identity-based access control is critical to prevent misuse and data exposure. [......
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New font-rendering trick hides malicious commands from AI tools
item.description: A new font-rendering attack causes AI assistants to miss malicious commands shown on webpages by hiding them in seemingly harmless HTML. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
